title: Undiscovered Gems In Asia To Explore This September 2025
content:
As global markets continue to respond to anticipated interest rate cuts and the ongoing artificial intelligence boom, Asian markets have shown resilience with notable gains in key indices such as Japan's Nikkei 225 and China's CSI 300. Amidst this backdrop of economic shifts and technological advancements, identifying promising small-cap stocks can be a strategic move for investors seeking opportunities in Asia's dynamic landscape.

Top 10 Undiscovered Gems With Strong Fundamentals In Asia

Let's explore several standout options from the results in the screener.

Dahon Tech (Shenzhen)

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Dahon Tech (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. is engaged in the development, design, manufacture, and marketing of folding bicycles and accessories, with a market capitalization of approximately HK$1.99 billion.

Operations: Dahon Tech's primary revenue stream is from its recreational vehicles segment, generating CN¥450.72 million. The company has a market capitalization of approximately HK$1.99 billion.

Dahon Tech (Shenzhen) recently made waves with an IPO, raising HKD 392.04 million by offering shares at HKD 49.5 each. This debt-free company showcases impressive financial health, with earnings growth of 50% over the past year, outpacing the leisure industry's -1%. Its stock trades at a discount of about 13% below estimated fair value, suggesting potential upside for investors. Despite high-quality earnings and positive free cash flow, its shares are highly illiquid, which could pose challenges for some investors looking to enter or exit positions quickly.

Cal-Comp Electronics (Thailand)

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Cal-Comp Electronics (Thailand) Public Company Limited, along with its subsidiaries, is engaged in the global manufacturing of electronic products and has a market capitalization of approximately THB63.75 billion.

Story Continues

Operations: Cal-Comp Electronics (Thailand) generates revenue primarily from computer peripherals and telecommunication products, with THB161.78 billion and THB23.83 billion respectively. Service income contributes an additional THB1.71 billion to the total revenue stream.

With a strong footing in the electronics industry, Cal-Comp Electronics (Thailand) has shown impressive earnings growth of 34.8% over the past year, outpacing its industry peers. Despite facing a drop in sales for August 2025 to THB 9 billion from THB 11.87 billion last year, the company remains profitable with free cash flow turning positive recently at THB 6.67 billion as of September 2024. The debt to equity ratio has improved significantly from 121.4% to 68% over five years, though it still carries a high net debt to equity ratio of 43.4%.

Navigate through the intricacies of Cal-Comp Electronics (Thailand) with our comprehensive health report here. Explore historical data to track Cal-Comp Electronics (Thailand)'s performance over time in our Past section.SET:CCET Earnings and Revenue Growth as at Sep 2025

Linktel Technologies

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Linktel Technologies Co., Ltd. is engaged in the research, development, production, and sale of optical transceiver modules both in China and internationally with a market capitalization of CN¥13.66 billion.

Operations: Linktel derives its revenue primarily from the optical communication industry, generating CN¥958.45 million. The company's net profit margin is not provided in the available data.

Linktel Technologies, a noteworthy player in the electronics industry, has seen its earnings grow by an impressive 251% over the past year, outpacing the sector's average of 3.8%. Despite this growth, its debt-to-equity ratio has increased from 8.9 to 34.3 over five years, suggesting rising leverage concerns. The company reported half-year sales of CNY 495 million and net income of CNY 34.8 million for June 2025, reflecting a solid performance compared to last year’s figures. Recent corporate governance changes include proposed amendments to their articles and abolishment of the supervisory committee, indicating strategic shifts ahead.
